title: The answer comes out
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The console shows five lines, but three of them are undefined. The numbers are coming from somewhere else.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
hint2.no-return: All three functions have to return. The first one already does.
hint2.no-total: The function prints the number instead of returning it. On screen it looks the same, but the result goes nowhere.
hint3.no-total: console.log shows. return sends the value back. The two have nothing in common.
hint4.no-total: Replace the printing with returning. The printing happens outside, where the function is called.
why.no-tip: The total with tip is not returned.
hint2.no-tip: The line calculates, but the result is thrown away immediately. Nobody asked for it back.
hint3.no-tip: total + tip on its own is an expression with no address. It is worked out and forgotten.
why.price-broken: The price is no longer a number.
hint2.price-broken: The first function was correct. If you changed it, put it back.
why.wrong-count: There are not three lines in the console.
hint2.wrong-count: The printing happens only at the end, three times. The functions do not print.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Three functions. The first returns a price by size. The second works out a total. The third adds a tip.
blocks.1.text: The console shows the numbers — 12, 10 — and then undefined three times. The numbers come from somewhere, but not from where they should.
blocks.2.text: It prints, but does not return
blocks.3.text: The second function calculates correctly and displays the result. That is exactly why it looks like it works.
blocks.4.text: But nothing comes out of it. Try to store the answer in a name and you get undefined. A function with no return always returns undefined.
blocks.5.quote: console.log shows you. return gives to the code. One is for your eyes, the other is for work.
blocks.7.text: The third function is nastier still. It calculates and does not even print — the line just sits there.
blocks.9.text: shout works out the capitals and forgets them immediately. result becomes undefined. Nothing breaks, nothing warns.
blocks.11.text: A function that prints does exactly one thing, and that thing is displaying. It cannot be used in a sum, cannot be passed to another function, cannot be stored.
blocks.12.text: A function that returns can do all of those. Displaying is one option — and it is decided outside, by whoever calls it.
blocks.14.text: Fix the second and third functions so they return instead of printing or throwing away.
blocks.15.text: The printing stays only in the three lines at the end. The functions print nothing.
